DISCOVERY 610, SUBMITTED ON THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF OMAR JAMES AND ROBERT JOHNSON IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE QUESTIONS CONCERNS MATTERS OF PUBLIC RECORD AND IS RELEVANT TO AN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AS WELL AS TO ASSESSING THE CREDIBILITY OF THE WITNESSES. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS THAT ANY INFORMATION OBTAINED FROM THE WITNESSES SHALL NOT BE USED FOR ANY PURPOSE UNRELATED TO THE PRESENT LITIGATION. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT MAY NOT DISCLOSE OR PERMIT TO BE DISCLOSED SUCH INFORMATION UNLESS SPECIFICALLY PERMITTED TO DO SO BY THE COURT AFTER A HEARING AND A SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE. NOTWITHSTANDING THE ABOVE, COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT MAY DISCLOSE OR PERMIT TO BE DISCLOSED INFORMATION OBTAINED DURING DISCOVERY TO PERSONS EMPLOYED BY COUNSEL TO ASSIST IN THE PREPARATION OF THE CASE, IF THAT DISCLOSURE IS REQUIRED FOR THAT PREPARATION. PERSONS PROVIDED THIS INFORMATION BY COUNSEL SHALL BE INFORMED THAT FURTHER DISSEMINATION OF THE INFORMATION CONSTITUTES A VIOLATION OF A COURT ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

DISCOVERY 610, RYAN KENT APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY FROM JASON ASHTON IS GRANTED. THE OBJECTIONS BASED ON RELEVANCY AND THE RIGHT TO PRIVACY MADE AT THE TIME OF THE DEPOSITION ARE OVERRULED. THE ASSERTION OF PRIVILEGE BASED UPON A CLAIM OF TRADE SECRETS IS UNTIMELY AS THE OBJECTION WAS NOT MADE DURING THE DEPOSITION AND WAS THEREFORE WAIVED. THE COURT DECLINES TO REQUIRE AN IN CAMERA REVIEW AS THE QUESTIONS SET FORTH IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT DO NOT ASK FOR POTENTIALLY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. ANY FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE SUBJECT TO THE PREVIOUS PROTECTIVE ORDER ISSUED BY THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (610/BCHAN)
